mnemonics: FLICK: Filter, Likelihood, Innovation, Covariance, Kalman
keyPoints: The likelihood is computed recursively using the Kalman Filter
The prediction error decomposition breaks the joint density into a product of one-step-ahead conditional densities
The innovations (one-step-ahead forecast errors) are a key component with their covariance matrix determining the likelihood contribution at each step
commonMistakes: Forgetting to account for the time-varying nature of the innovation covariance matrix
Assuming the prediction errors are serially correlated when they should be uncorrelated under correct model specification
Mishandling the initial state distribution which can lead to biased likelihood estimates
